metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_995
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Authors investigate the cellular localization of the actin-binding protein cortactin in rat brain using confocal imaging and immuno EM. Confocal imaging revealed that cortactin immunoreactivity is localized apposed to the presynaptic protein synaptophysin and Vglut, which suggests its presence in the postsynaptic compartment of excitatory synapses. Co-staining with a lipophilic dye that labels plasma membrane revealed the accumulation of cortactin in dendritic spines. Electron microscopy with immunoperoxidase labeling of cortactin revealed staining in dendrites which was denser within dendritic spines below PSDs, confirming the localization of cortactin within spines. In order to gain insight into the precise location of the protein, immunogold EM was performed. In agreement with previous results, immunogold labeling was prominent in spines, usually associated with electron-dense filaments, which suggests interaction with cytoskeleton. Analysis of the distribution of the immunogold particles within the spines revealed that these are localized 100-150nm away from the synapse, in the spine core.

11/8/2017 Pim
- Based on prior knowledge of Cortactin and the statement "... usually associated with electron-dense filaments..." I allow annotation to the term 'postsynaptic actin cytoskeleton (GO:0098871)' without an IC construct.. Experimental description: "Experiments were performed on adult (3-5 months old) male Sprague Dawley rats from Charles River (Raleigh, NC)."
